name: Block
description: A defensive state that reduces or prevents incoming damage within defined limits.
design_notes: Block is a defensive baseline that must coexist with dodge, parry, armor, and positioning. It needs clear rules for guard direction, chip damage, stamina drain, guard break, and unblockable attacks. If the player cannot tell why a block failed, the system will feel unfair even when technically correct.
design_purpose: Give players a reliable defensive option against readable pressure.
Create resource and positioning tradeoffs through stamina, guard angle, or chip damage.
Support enemy move design with blockable, guard-breaking, and unblockable categories.
player_fantasy: The player feels braced and protected, absorbing danger through preparation instead of movement.

parameters.name: guard_arc_degrees
parameters.description: Facing cone in which incoming attacks can be blocked.

parameters.name: block_damage_reduction_ratio
parameters.description: Share of eligible incoming damage prevented by guard.

parameters.name: guard_stamina_cost_per_hit
parameters.description: Stamina spent when a blocked attack lands.

parameters.name: guard_raise_ms
parameters.description: Delay before a new block state becomes fully protective.

parameters.name: chip_damage_ratio
parameters.description: Share of blocked damage that still affects health.

edge_cases: Area attacks and explosions need rules for whether guard angle or distance matters more.
Blocking while airborne, crouched, stunned, climbing, or reloading should have explicit priority.
Guard break at zero stamina must clear block state before applying follow-up hit reactions.
Multiple simultaneous attackers should not multiply stamina drain unintentionally unless designed.
common_bugs: Block checks use the attacker's facing instead of the defender's guard arc.
Stamina reaches zero after damage reduction, allowing one extra free block.
Hold block remains active after weapon swap or animation cancel.
Client and server calculate different guard angles because rotation smoothing is presentation-only.
balancing_notes: High reduction and low stamina cost make block dominant; chip damage and guard break preserve pressure.
Enemies need visible answers to block, but unblockable attacks should be signaled before commitment.
accessibility_notes: Support hold and toggle guard if blocking is sustained for long encounters.
Use distinct indicators for blockable, guard-breaking, and unblockable attacks where readability is critical.

implementation_notes.unity: Model startup, active, recovery, and cancel windows as data, then drive hitboxes, animation events, and damage application from one combat state source. For Block, validate this path with parameters such as guard_arc_degrees and block_damage_reduction_ratio.
implementation_notes.godot: Use Area2D or Area3D hit volumes with frame-window state on the actor; avoid placing damage authority inside animation-only callbacks. For Block, validate this path with parameters such as guard_arc_degrees and block_damage_reduction_ratio.
implementation_notes.unreal: Implement the action as a Gameplay Ability or Actor Component with montage notifies for windows and authoritative damage on the server when networked. For Block, validate this path with parameters such as guard_arc_degrees and block_damage_reduction_ratio.
implementation_notes.web: Update combat windows in the fixed simulation step, evaluate hurtbox overlap from cached transforms, and emit presentation events separately from damage state. For Block, validate this path with parameters such as guard_arc_degrees and block_damage_reduction_ratio.
